---
title: "AgriCerrado Calibration Report"
author: "João Marcolin"
date: "28/04/2026"
output: html_document
---

```{r preamble, include=FALSE}
# loads everything we need from main.R so I don't have to go back to that script
rm(list=ls())
library(tidyverse)
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(exactextractr)
library(readxl)
library(tictoc)

param_plot_km <- 5
set_subset    <- FALSE
set_name      <- "brazil"

report_states <- c("GO", "MT", "MS", "MA", "TO", "PI", "BA", "DF", "AC", "AP", "AM", "PA", "RO", "RR")
```


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, out.width='800px')

library(magrittr)
require(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(pander)
library(reshape2)
library(kableExtra)
library(viridis)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
theme_set(theme_grey())

## define auxiliary function to save figures
# picks a folder to save the figures (if necessary, create the folder)
if (!dir.exists("figures")) dir.create("figures")
# auxiliary function for saving figures
save_fig <- function(plot, name, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 500) {
  ggsave(paste0("figures/", name, ".png"), plot = plot, width = width, height = height, dpi = dpi)
}

```

```{r load}
# load inputs
df_brazil_mun    <- readr::read_csv(
  "data_outputs/2_brazil_mun/df_brazil_mun.csv",
  col_types = cols(
    .default               = col_character(),
    legal_amazon           = col_logical(),
    amazon_area_km2        = col_number(),
    atlantic_area_km2      = col_number(),
    caatinga_area_km2      = col_number(),
    cerrado_area_km2       = col_number(),
    islands_area_km2       = col_number(),
    pampa_area_km2         = col_number(),
    pantanal_area_km2      = col_number(),
    mun_area_km2           = col_number(),
    mun_protected_area_km2 = col_number(),
    FAO_soy_yield          = col_number()
    )
  )

df_census_state  <- readr::read_csv(
  "data_outputs/3_census_data/df_census_state.csv",
  col_types = cols(
    .default      = col_character(),
    year          = col_number(),
    farm_area_ha  = col_number(),
    n_farms       = col_number(),
    activity_area = col_number(),
    workers       = col_number()
    )
  )

df_yearly_mun    <- readr::read_csv(
  "data_outputs/4_yearly_data/df_yearly_mun.csv",
  col_types = cols(
    .default = col_character(),
    legal_amazon   = col_logical(),
    year           = col_number(),
    pam_area_ha    = col_number(),
    pam_output_ton = col_number(),
    ppm_herd       = col_number(),
    mb_natcover_ha = col_number(),
    mb_nonveg_ha   = col_number(),
    mb_notobs_ha   = col_number(),
    mb_pasture_ha  = col_number(),
    mb_soybean_ha  = col_number(),
    mb_tcrop_ha    = col_number(),
    mb_forestry_ha = col_number(),
    mb_pcrop_ha    = col_number()
    )
  )

df_yearly_prices <- readr::read_csv(
  "data_outputs/4_yearly_data/df_prices.csv",
  col_types = cols(
    .default = col_number()
    )
  )

```

# Output prices, minimum wage and interest rate

```{r yearly_prices, echo=FALSE}

# auxiliary function to plot yearly values (line plot)
plot_var <- function(df, var, var_name, title) {
  ggplot(df, aes(x=year, y=.data[[var]])) +
    geom_line() +
    labs(title=title, x="Year", y=var_name) +
    theme_minimal()
}

# fix NAs in price_C and price_S
df_yearly_prices$price_C[1:10] <- NA
df_yearly_prices$price_S[1:10] <- NA

# plot
plot_interest <- plot_var(df_yearly_prices, "interest", "interest", "Cumulative yearly interest rate") #+
                   #geom_smooth(method = "loess", se=FALSE) # add trend line
plot_wage     <- plot_var(df_yearly_prices, "wage",     "min. wage", "National minimum wage") #+
                   #geom_smooth(method = "loess", se=FALSE) # add trend line
plot_price_C  <- plot_var(df_yearly_prices, "price_C",  "BRL per ton", "Beef price (base=2024)") #+
                   #geom_smooth(method = "loess", se=FALSE) # add trend line
plot_price_S  <- plot_var(df_yearly_prices, "price_S",  "BRL per ton", "Soybean price (base=2024)") #+
                   #geom_smooth(method = "loess", se=FALSE) # add trend line

# display plots
(plot_interest | plot_wage) / (plot_price_C | plot_price_S)

# save plots
save_fig(plot_interest, "Brazil_interest")
save_fig(plot_wage,     "Brazil_wage")
save_fig(plot_price_C,  "Brazil_price_C")
save_fig(plot_price_S,  "Brazil_price_S")

# fill NAs back
df_yearly_prices$price_C[1:10] <- df_yearly_prices$price_C[11]
df_yearly_prices$price_S[1:10] <- df_yearly_prices$price_S[11]

rm(plot_interest, plot_wage, plot_price_C, plot_price_S, df_yearly_prices2)
```

# Beef and Soybean yield
## National and State-level trends

```{r yearly_agg_yield, echo=FALSE}
# summarise at the state level
yearly_state_data <- df_yearly_mun %>%
  dplyr::select(
    year, state,
    mb_pasture_ha, mb_soybean_ha,
    ppm_herd, pam_output_ton, pam_area_ha,
    ) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(
    state, year
    ) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    across(everything(), ~sum(.x,na.rm=TRUE))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    yield_C = (ppm_herd*0.1*0.4)/mb_pasture_ha,
    yield_S = pam_output_ton/pam_area_ha
  )

# summarise at the national level
yearly_brazil_data <- df_yearly_mun %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    year, state = "Brazil",
    mb_pasture_ha, mb_soybean_ha,
    ppm_herd, pam_output_ton, pam_area_ha,
    ) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(
    state, year
    ) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    across(everything(), ~sum(.x,na.rm=TRUE))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    yield_C = (ppm_herd*0.1*0.4)/mb_pasture_ha,
    yield_S = pam_output_ton/pam_area_ha
  )
# fix yield_S_mb: it's way too high in 1985
yearly_brazil_data$yield_S_mb[1] <- yearly_brazil_data$yield_S_mb[2]

# plot country-level data
plot_yield_C <- plot_var(yearly_brazil_data, "yield_C", "tons per ha.", "Beef yield (Brazil)") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se=FALSE) # add trend line
plot_yield_S <- plot_var(yearly_brazil_data, "yield_S", "tons per ha.", "Soybean yield (Brazil)") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se=FALSE) # add trend line

# plot state-level data
# plot yield_C
plot_yield_C_state <- ggplot(yearly_state_data, aes(x=year, y=yield_C, color=state)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title="Beef yield", x="Year", y="tons/ha", color="State") +
  theme_minimal()

# plot yield_S
plot_yield_S_state <- ggplot(yearly_state_data, aes(x=year, y=yield_S, color=state)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title="Soybean yield (mb)", x="Year", y="tons/ha", color="State") +
  theme_minimal()

(plot_yield_C | plot_yield_S) /
(plot_yield_C_state | plot_yield_S_state)

save_fig(plot_yield_C, "Brazil_yield_C")
save_fig(plot_yield_S, "Brazil_yield_S")

save_fig(plot_yield_C_state, "states_yield_C")
save_fig(plot_yield_S_state, "states_yield_S")

rm(plot_yield_C, plot_yield_S,
   plot_yield_C_state, plot_yield_S_state,
   yearly_brazil_data, yearly_state_data)


```


```{r yearly_mun_yield, echo=FALSE}
## auxiliary function to summarise by municipality group
#summarise_mun_yield_f <- function(yr) {
#  # define grouping column
#  group_col <- paste0("group_",yr)
#  # summarise df_yearly_mun
#  df_year <- df_yearly_mun %>%
#    dplyr::select(
#      all_of(group_col), year,
#      pam_area_ha, pam_output_ton,
#      ppm_herd, mb_pasture_ha
#      ) %>%
#    dplyr::group_by(
#      across(all_of(group_col)),
#      year
#      ) %>%
#    dplyr::summarise(
#      across(everything(), ~sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
#      .groups = "drop"
#      ) %>%
#    dplyr::mutate(
#      area_C   = mb_pasture_ha,
#      output_C = ppm_herd*0.1*0.4,
#      area_S   = pam_area_ha,
#      output_S = pam_output_ton
#    ) %>%
#    dplyr::filter(
#      year >= as.integer(yr)
#    )
#  # calculate yield
#  df_year$yield_C <- ifelse(df_year$area_C > 0,
#                            df_year$output_C / df_year$area_C,
#                            0)
#  df_year$yield_S <- ifelse(df_year$area_S > 0,
#                            df_year$output_S/df_year$area_S,
#                            0)
#  # return summarised data.frame
#  return(df_year)
#} # close summarise_mun_yield_f(yr)
#
## summarise by municipality group
#mun_1985_yearly_data <- summarise_mun_yield_f("1985")
#mun_1995_yearly_data <- summarise_mun_yield_f("1995")
#mun_2006_yearly_data <- summarise_mun_yield_f("2006")
#mun_2017_yearly_data <- summarise_mun_yield_f("2017")
#rm(summarise_mun_yield_f)
#
## very few 0 values for cattle, it's probably safe to use the minimum productivity as the lower bound
#sum(mun_1985_yearly_data$yield_C==0) # 130
#sum(mun_1995_yearly_data$yield_C==0) # 116
#sum(mun_2006_yearly_data$yield_C==0) # 50
#sum(mun_2017_yearly_data$yield_C==0) # 10
#
## too many 0 values for soybean - need to deal with this somehow
#sum(mun_1985_yearly_data$yield_S==0) # 27794
#sum(mun_1995_yearly_data$yield_S==0) # 26285
#sum(mun_2006_yearly_data$yield_S==0) # 22063
#sum(mun_2017_yearly_data$yield_S==0) #  8454
#
## there are a few extreme values in the IBGE data, it's probably best to use FAO GAEZ's attainable yield for soybean
#summary(df_brazil_mun$FAO_soy_yield)
#summary(mun_1985_yearly_data$yield_S[mun_1985_yearly_data$yield_S>0])
#summary(mun_1995_yearly_data$yield_S[mun_1995_yearly_data$yield_S>0])
#summary(mun_2006_yearly_data$yield_S[mun_2006_yearly_data$yield_S>0])
#summary(mun_2017_yearly_data$yield_S[mun_2017_yearly_data$yield_S>0])
#
## clean up
#rm(mun_1985_yearly_data, mun_1995_yearly_data,
#   mun_2006_yearly_data, mun_2017_yearly_data)
```

## Distribution of municipality-level yield

```{r mun_yield_distribution_years, echo=FALSE}
# auxiliary function to summarise by municipality group
summarise_mun_yield_f <- function(yr) {
  # define grouping column
  group_col <- paste0("group_",yr)
  # summarise df_yearly_mun
  df_year <- df_yearly_mun %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      state %in% report_states,
      year == yr) %>%
    dplyr::select(
      all_of(group_col), state, year,
      pam_area_ha, pam_output_ton,
      ppm_herd, mb_pasture_ha
      ) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(
      across(all_of(group_col)),
      state,
      year
      ) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      across(everything(), ~sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
      .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      area_C   = mb_pasture_ha,
      output_C = ppm_herd*0.1*0.4,
      area_S   = pam_area_ha,
      output_S = pam_output_ton
    )
  # calculate yield
  df_year$yield_C <- ifelse(df_year$area_C > 0,
                            df_year$output_C / df_year$area_C,
                            0)
  df_year$yield_S <- ifelse(df_year$area_S > 0,
                            df_year$output_S/df_year$area_S,
                            0)
  ## get code_mun back to data.frame
  #df_year <- df_yearly_mun %>%
  #  dplyr::select(code_mun, all_of(group_col)) %>%
  #  dplyr::left_join(df_year, by=group_col) %>%
  #  dplyr::select(code_mun, year, area_C:yield_S)
  # return summarised data.frame
  return(df_year)
} # close summarise_mun_yield_f(yr)

# summarise by municipality group
mun_1985_yearly_data <- summarise_mun_yield_f("1985") %>% rename(mun = group_1985)
mun_1995_yearly_data <- summarise_mun_yield_f("1995") %>% rename(mun = group_1995)
mun_2006_yearly_data <- summarise_mun_yield_f("2006") %>% rename(mun = group_2006)
mun_2017_yearly_data <- summarise_mun_yield_f("2017") %>% rename(mun = group_2017)
rm(summarise_mun_yield_f)

mun_yields_years <- rbind(mun_1985_yearly_data, mun_1995_yearly_data,
                          mun_2006_yearly_data, mun_2017_yearly_data)

# plot dispersion of yield_C
plot_box_C_years <- mun_yields_years %>%
  dplyr::filter(area_C > 0) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(x = factor(year), y = yield_C, fill = factor(year))
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size=0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("1985"="#a38b00","1995"="#c9b84a","2006"="#e0cf7a","2017"="#edde8e"),
                    guide="none") +
  facet_wrap(~state, ncol=4, scales="free_y") +
  labs(title    = "Beef yield distribution across years",
       subtitle = paste0("Municipalities with pasture area > 0 ha"),
       x="Year", y="tons / ha") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1))

# plot dispersion of yield_S_mb
plot_box_S_years <- mun_yields_years %>%
  dplyr::filter(area_S > 0) %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(x = factor(year), y = yield_S, fill = factor(year))
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size=0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("1985"="#7b3b63","1995"="#9e5c84","2006"="#b56d95","2017"="#C27BA0"),
                    guide="none") +
  facet_wrap(~state, ncol=4, scales="free_y") +
  labs(title    = "Soybean yield distribution across years",
       subtitle = paste0("Municipalities with soy area > 0 ha"),
       x="Year", y="tons / ha") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1))

(plot_box_C_years | plot_box_S_years)

save_fig(plot_box_C_years, "states_theta_C_box_plot_yearly")
save_fig(plot_box_S_years, "states_theta_S_box_plot_yearly")

rm(mun_yields, mun_yields_years,
   plot_box_C_years, plot_box_S_years)
```

```{r mun_yield_distribution_fao, echo=FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
# default fig.width=7, making the figure wider must make it easier to plot the scales

# municipality-level yields
mun_yields <- df_brazil_mun %>%
  dplyr::filter(state %in% report_states) %>%
  dplyr::select(code_mun, state, mun_area_km2, FAO_soy_yield)

# plot beef yield distribution
plot_box_S_fao <- mun_yields %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(x = reorder(state, FAO_soy_yield, FUN=median), y = FAO_soy_yield)
    ) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size=0.8, fill="#C27BA0") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title  = paste0("Soybean attainable yield distribution by state (FAO GAEZ)"),
       x = NULL, y = "tons / ha") +
  theme_minimal()

plot_box_S_fao

save_fig(plot_box_S_fao,"states_yield_S_fao_box_plot")

rm(plot_box_C, plot_box_S_fao)
```

# Land Use and Cover Change

```{r yearly_LULC, echo=FALSE}

# (1) first I generate a plot with all LULC categories from yearly_data
yearly_LULC_data <- df_yearly_mun %>%
  dplyr::select(year, mb_natcover_ha, mb_nonveg_ha, mb_notobs_ha, mb_pasture_ha,
                mb_soybean_ha, mb_tcrop_ha, mb_forestry_ha, mb_pcrop_ha) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(year) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
                   .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(total_area = mb_natcover_ha + mb_nonveg_ha + mb_notobs_ha + mb_pasture_ha +
                  mb_soybean_ha + mb_tcrop_ha + mb_forestry_ha + mb_pcrop_ha) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(year,
                   mb_natcover_ha = 100 * mb_natcover_ha / total_area,
                   mb_nonveg_ha   = 100 * mb_nonveg_ha   / total_area,
                   mb_notobs_ha   = 100 * mb_notobs_ha   / total_area,
                   mb_pasture_ha  = 100 * mb_pasture_ha  / total_area,
                   mb_soybean_ha  = 100 * mb_soybean_ha  / total_area,
                   mb_tcrop_ha    = 100 * mb_tcrop_ha    / total_area,
                   mb_forestry_ha = 100 * mb_forestry_ha / total_area,
                   mb_pcrop_ha    = 100 * mb_pcrop_ha    / total_area)

lulc_order <- c("mb_natcover_ha", "mb_forestry_ha", "mb_pasture_ha",
                "mb_soybean_ha", "mb_tcrop_ha", "mb_pcrop_ha",
                "mb_nonveg_ha", "mb_notobs_ha"
                )

plot_LULC1 <- yearly_LULC_data %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols=-year, names_to="category",values_to="value") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(category=factor(category,levels=lulc_order)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=year, y=value, fill=category)) +
  geom_area() +
 scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "mb_natcover_ha" = "#1f8d49",
      "mb_forestry_ha" = "#7a5900",
      "mb_pasture_ha"  = "#edde8e",
      "mb_soybean_ha"  = "#f5b3c8",
      "mb_tcrop_ha"    = "#C27BA0",
      "mb_pcrop_ha"    = "#d082de",
      "mb_nonveg_ha"   = "#d4271e",
      "mb_notobs_ha"   = "#838B8B"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "mb_natcover_ha" = "Natural cover",
      "mb_forestry_ha" = "Forestry",
      "mb_pasture_ha"  = "Pasture",
      "mb_soybean_ha"  = "Soybean",
      "mb_tcrop_ha"    = "Temporary crops",
      "mb_pcrop_ha"    = "Perennial crops",
      "mb_nonveg_ha"   = "Non-vegetated",
      "mb_notobs_ha"   = "Not observed"
    )
  ) +
  labs(title="Yearly LULC shares",
       x="Year",
       y="Share of total area (%)",
       fill="Land use category") +
  theme_minimal()


# (2) I also generate a simplified LULC plot, containing only the LULC categories present in the model
yearly_LULC2 <- yearly_LULC_data %>%
  dplyr::transmute(year,
                   veg = mb_natcover_ha + mb_forestry_ha,
                   cattle = mb_pasture_ha,
                   crop = mb_soybean_ha + mb_tcrop_ha + mb_pcrop_ha,
                   abandoned = mb_nonveg_ha + mb_notobs_ha)

lulc_order2 <- c("veg", "cattle", "crop", "abandoned")

plot_LULC2 <- yearly_LULC2 %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols=-year, names_to="category",values_to="value") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(category=factor(category,levels=lulc_order2)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=year, y=value, fill=category)) +
  geom_area() +
 scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "veg"       = "#1f8d49",
      "cattle"    = "#edde8e",
      "crop"      = "#C27BA0",
      "abandoned" = "#d4271e"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "veg"       = "Vegetation",
      "cattle"    = "Pasture",
      "crop"      = "Crops",
      "abandoned" = "Non-vegetated"
    )
  ) +
  labs(title="Yearly LULC shares (simplified)",
       x="Year",
       y="Share of total area (%)",
       fill="Land use category") +
  theme_minimal()

# show plots
(plot_LULC1 | plot_LULC2)
save_fig(plot_LULC1,"Brazil_lulc_8classes")
save_fig(plot_LULC2,"Brazil_lulc_4classes")

# clean up
rm(yearly_data2, yearly_LULC_data, yearly_LULC2, lulc_order, lulc_order2, yearly_data, plot_var,
   plot_LULC1, plot_LULC2)

```


```{r yearly_LULC_by_state, echo=FALSE}
# get lulc variables for state-year pairs
lulc_state <- df_yearly_mun %>%
  dplyr::filter(state %in% report_states) %>%
  dplyr::select(state, year, mb_natcover_ha, mb_nonveg_ha, mb_notobs_ha, mb_pasture_ha,
                mb_soybean_ha, mb_tcrop_ha, mb_forestry_ha, mb_pcrop_ha) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(state,year) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
                   .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(total_area = mb_natcover_ha + mb_nonveg_ha + mb_notobs_ha + mb_pasture_ha +
                  mb_soybean_ha + mb_tcrop_ha + mb_forestry_ha + mb_pcrop_ha) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(year, state,
                   veg       = (mb_natcover_ha + mb_forestry_ha)/total_area,
                   cattle    = (mb_pasture_ha)/total_area,
                   crop      = (mb_soybean_ha + mb_tcrop_ha + mb_pcrop_ha)/total_area,
                   abandoned = (mb_nonveg_ha + mb_notobs_ha)/total_area
                   ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols=c(veg,cattle,crop,abandoned),
                      names_to="category", values_to="share") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(category=factor(category, levels=c("veg","cattle","crop","abandoned")))

# plot
plot_lulc_state <- ggplot(lulc_state, aes(x=year, y=share, fill=category)) +
  geom_area() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("veg"="#1f8d49", "cattle"="#edde8e",
               "crop"="#C27BA0", "abandoned"="#d4271e"),
    labels = c("veg"="Vegetation", "cattle"="Pasture",
               "crop"="Crops",     "abandoned"="Non-vegetated")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent) +
  facet_wrap(~state, ncol=4) +
  labs(title="Yearly LULC shares by Cerrado state (simplified)",
       x="Year", y="Share of total area", fill="Category") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45, hjust=1))

plot_lulc_state
save_fig(plot_lulc_state,"states_lulc")

rm(lulc_state, plot_lulc_state)

```

<!--
# Labor intensity

Using data from IBGE's Census of Agriculture (available for 1985, 1995, 2006, and 2017) at the state level, I calculate the number of workers per hectare in three sectors of activity: cattle ranching, temporary crops and forestry. Below, I provide the numbers for states with large Cerrado areas and the country as a whole:

```{r labor_intensity_ha, echo=FALSE}

# state-level data
labor_intensity_state <- workers_per_ha %>%
  dplyr::filter(state %in% cerrado_states,
                activity_group %in% c("cattle", "temp_crop")) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(state, activity_group, year) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(workers = sum(workers, na.rm=TRUE),
                   area    = sum(area,    na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(workers_ha = workers/area) %>%
  dplyr::select(state, activity_group, year, workers_ha) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from=c(activity_group,year),
                     values_from=workers_ha)

# national-level data
labor_intensity_national <- workers_per_ha %>%
  dplyr::filter(activity_group %in% c("cattle","temp_crop")) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(activity_group, year) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(workers = sum(workers, na.rm=TRUE),
                   area    = sum(area,    na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(workers_ha = workers/area,
                state      = "Brazil") %>%
  dplyr::select(state, activity_group, year, workers_ha) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from=c(activity_group, year),
                     values_from=workers_ha)

# bind and order columns
table_wph <- base::rbind(labor_intensity_state, labor_intensity_national) %>%
  dplyr::select(state,
                cattle_1995, temp_crop_1995,
                cattle_2006, temp_crop_2006,
                cattle_2017, temp_crop_2017)

# generate table
table_wph %>%
  kableExtra::kable(col.names=c("Area", "Cattle", "Crops", "Cattle", "Crops", "Cattle", "Crops"),
                    digits=4,
                    caption=paste0("Workers per Hectare by Sector")) %>%
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" "=1, "1995"=2, "2006"=2, "2017"=2)) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                            full_width=FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(table_wph),bold=TRUE)

rm(labor_intensity_state, labor_intensity_national)

```

Since we are working with plots much larger than 1 hectare, at the cell level we have

```{r labor_intensity_plots, echo=FALSE}

if (param_plot_km == 1) {
  # get values per 100 hectares (1 sq km)
  table_wph[,2:7] <- table_wph[,2:7]*100
} else if (param_plot_km == 5) {
  # get values per 2500 hectares (25 sq km)
  table_wph[,2:7] <- table_wph[,2:7]*2500
}

# generate table
table_wph %>%
  kableExtra::kable(col.names=c("Area", "Cattle", "Crops", "Cattle", "Crops", "Cattle", "Crops"),
                    digits=4,
                    caption=paste0("Workers per Plot (",param_plot_km,"km x ",param_plot_km,"km) by Sector")) %>%
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" "=1, "1995"=2, "2006"=2, "2017"=2)) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                            full_width=FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(table_wph),bold=TRUE)

rm(table_wph)
```


# Land Concentration

Using data from IBGE's Census of Agriculture (available for 1995, 2006, and 2017) at the state level, I calculate the share of farm area in each of the following area classes:
- less than 100 hectares;
- from 100 to 1000 hectares;
- more than 1000 hectares.
This data is available at the state level, but I also provide results for the whole country

```{r land_concentration_all_years, echo=FALSE}

# labels size class
size_class_labels <- c(
  "less_than_100_ha"    = "< 100 ha",
  "from_100_to_1000_ha" = "100–1000 ha",
  "more_than_1000_ha"   = "> 1000 ha"
)

# helper function to compute shares from a raw area/farms table
get_shares <- function(df, states) {
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% states) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(year) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(less_than_100_ha    = sum(less_than_100_ha,    na.rm=TRUE),
                     from_100_to_1000_ha = sum(from_100_to_1000_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                     more_than_1000_ha   = sum(more_than_1000_ha,   na.rm=TRUE),
                     total               = sum(total,               na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::transmute(year,
                     less_than_100_ha    = 100 * less_than_100_ha    / total,
                     from_100_to_1000_ha = 100 * from_100_to_1000_ha / total,
                     more_than_1000_ha   = 100 * more_than_1000_ha   / total)
    return(df)
} # closes get_share()

# helper function to build wide table (states as rows, year-class as columns)
build_wide <- function(df_list) {
  # df_list: named list of per-state/national share tables
  base::do.call(base::rbind, lapply(base::names(df_list), function(nm) {
    df_list[[nm]] %>%
      tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = less_than_100_ha:more_than_1000_ha,
                          names_to  = "size_class",
                          values_to = "value") %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from  = c(size_class, year),
                         values_from = value) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(area = nm)
  })) %>%
    dplyr::select(
      area,
      less_than_100_ha_1985,    from_100_to_1000_ha_1985,    more_than_1000_ha_1985,
      less_than_100_ha_1995,    from_100_to_1000_ha_1995,    more_than_1000_ha_1995,
      less_than_100_ha_2006,    from_100_to_1000_ha_2006,    more_than_1000_ha_2006,
      less_than_100_ha_2017,    from_100_to_1000_ha_2017,    more_than_1000_ha_2017
    )
} # close build_wide()

# Table 1) area shares
area_by_state <- lapply(cerrado_states, function(s) get_shares(farm_area_by_class, s))
base::names(area_by_state) <- cerrado_states
area_national    <- list("Brazil" = get_shares(farm_area_by_class, unique(farm_area_by_class$state)))
area_wide        <- build_wide(c(area_by_state, area_national))

area_wide %>%
  kableExtra::kable(col.names=c("Area",
                                "<100", "100-1000", ">1000",
                                "<100", "100-1000", ">1000",
                                "<100", "100-1000", ">1000",
                                "<100", "100-1000", ">1000"),
                    digits=1,
                    caption="Share of total farm area (%) by size class") %>%
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" "=1, "1985"=3, "1995"=3, "2006"=3, "2017"=3)) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("stripend", "hover", "condensed"),
                            full_width=FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(area_wide),bold=TRUE)

# Table 2) farm count shares
farms_by_state <- lapply(cerrado_states, function(s) get_shares(farm_number_by_class, s))
base::names(farms_by_state) <- cerrado_states
farms_national   <- list("Brazil" = get_shares(farm_number_by_class, unique(farm_number_by_class$state)))
farms_wide       <- build_wide(c(farms_by_state, farms_national))

farms_wide %>%
  kableExtra::kable(col.names=c("Area",
                                "<100", "100-1000", ">1000",
                                "<100", "100-1000", ">1000",
                                "<100", "100-1000", ">1000",
                                "<100", "100-1000", ">1000"),
                    digits=1,
                    caption="Share of total farm count (%) by size class") %>%
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" "=1, "1985"=3, "1995"=3, "2006"=3, "2017"=3)) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                            full_width=FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(farms_wide), bold=TRUE)

# Plot 1) stacked bar chart
# national area shares by size class (19985, 1995, 2006, 2017)
chart_data <- farm_area_by_class %>%
  dplyr::group_by(year) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(less_than_100_ha    = sum(less_than_100_ha,    na.rm=TRUE),
                   from_100_to_1000_ha = sum(from_100_to_1000_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                   more_than_1000_ha   = sum(more_than_1000_ha,   na.rm=TRUE),
                   total               = sum(total,               na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::transmute(year,
                  `< 100 ha`       = 100 * less_than_100_ha    / total,
                  `100–1000 ha`    = 100 * from_100_to_1000_ha / total,
                  `> 1000 ha`      = 100 * more_than_1000_ha   / total) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -year,
                      names_to  = "size_class",
                      values_to = "share") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(size_class = factor(size_class,
                                    levels = c("< 100 ha", "100–1000 ha", "> 1000 ha")))

g_land_concentration <- chart_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=year, y=share, fill=size_class)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("< 100 ha"    = "#a6cee3",
                               "100–1000 ha" = "#1f78b4",
                               "> 1000 ha"   = "#081d58")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  labs(title   = "National farm area by size class",
       x       = "Census year",
       y       = "Share of total farmland area",
       fill    = "Size class") +
  theme_minimal()

gridExtra::grid.arrange(g_land_concentration)

save_fig(g_land_concentration,"Brazil_land_concentration")

# clean up
rm(area_by_state, area_national, area_wide,
   farms_by_state, farms_national, farms_wide,
   get_shares, build_wide,
   chart_data, g_land_concentration)
  
```

The 2017 Census of Agriculture has more classes of farm size, so we can take a more detailed look in that year:

```{r land_concentration_2017, echo=FALSE}
# correct for the "landless" category in the 2017 census
# for the area table, we just need to remove the "landless" column (area is 0 anyway)
area_table_2017 <- farm_area_by_class_2017 %>%
  dplyr::select(state, less_than_100_ha, from_100_to_1000_ha, from_1000_to_2500_ha,
                from_2500_to_10000_ha, more_than_10000_ha, total)
# for the farm number table, we also need to recalculate the total number of farms
farm_table_2017 <- farm_number_by_class_2017 %>%
  dplyr::transmute(state, less_than_100_ha, from_100_to_1000_ha, from_1000_to_2500_ha, from_2500_to_10000_ha, more_than_10000_ha,
                   total = less_than_100_ha+from_100_to_1000_ha+from_1000_to_2500_ha+from_2500_to_10000_ha+more_than_10000_ha)

# helper function to compute shares from 2017 raw area/farms table
get_shares <- function(df, states) {
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% states) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(less_than_100_ha      = sum(less_than_100_ha,      na.rm=TRUE),
                     from_100_to_1000_ha   = sum(from_100_to_1000_ha,   na.rm=TRUE),
                     from_1000_to_2500_ha  = sum(from_1000_to_2500_ha,  na.rm=TRUE),
                     from_2500_to_10000_ha = sum(from_2500_to_10000_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                     more_than_10000_ha    = sum(more_than_10000_ha,     na.rm=TRUE),
                     total                 = sum(total,                  na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(less_than_100_ha      = 100 * less_than_100_ha      / total,
                     from_100_to_1000_ha   = 100 * from_100_to_1000_ha   / total,
                     from_1000_to_2500_ha  = 100 * from_1000_to_2500_ha  / total,
                     from_2500_to_10000_ha = 100 * from_2500_to_10000_ha / total,
                     more_than_10000_ha    = 100 * more_than_10000_ha     / total)
  return(df)
} # closes get_shares()

# helper function to build wide table (states as rows, year-class as columns)
build_wide <- function(df, states) {
  all_states <- unique(df$state)
  # state rows
  state_rows <- lapply(states, function(s) {
    get_shares(df, s) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(area = s, .before = 1)
  })
  # national row
  national_row <- get_shares(df, all_states) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(area = "Brazil (national)", .before = 1)
  # bind
  base::do.call(base::rbind, c(state_rows, list(national_row)))
}

# Table 1) area shares
area_wide <- build_wide(area_table_2017, cerrado_states)
area_wide %>%
  kableExtra::kable(col.names=c("Area","< 100 ha","100–1000 ha","1000–2500 ha","2500–10000 ha","> 10000 ha"),
                    digits=1,
                    caption="Share of total farm area (%) by size class (2017)") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                            full_width=FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(area_wide),bold=TRUE)

# Table 2) farm count shares
farms_wide <- build_wide(farm_table_2017, cerrado_states)
farms_wide %>%
  kableExtra::kable(col.names=c("Area","< 100 ha","100–1000 ha","1000–2500 ha","2500–10000 ha","> 10000 ha"),
                    digits=1,
                    caption="Share of total farm count (%) by size class (2017)") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                            full_width = FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(farms_wide),bold=TRUE)


# clean up
rm(get_shares, build_wide,
   area_wide, farms_wide)

```

We can use this data to get rough estimates for the values of parameters `share_smallprod`, `share_mediumprod`, `share_largeprod`, `ratio_medium_to_small`, and `ratio_large_to_small`. If the cell grid has 1km resolution, each cell has 100 hectares and we can initialize the model with values from 1985, 1995, 2006 or 2017 with no issues. If the cell grid has 5km resolution, each cell has 2500 hectares and only the 2017 data has a 1-1 relation with AgriCerrado's grid structure - we can still use the data from 1985, 1995 or 2006, but we must be aware that this is a gross approximation.

```{r land_concentration_parameters, echo=FALSE}
# helper function to compute the 5 parameters for a given set of states and year
# small  = <100ha
# medium = 100-1000ha
# large  = >1000ha

get_params_3class <- function(states, yr) {
  farm_number <- farm_number_by_class %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% states, year==yr) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(small  = sum(less_than_100_ha,    na.rm=TRUE),
                     medium = sum(from_100_to_1000_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                     large  = sum(more_than_1000_ha,   na.rm=TRUE),
                     total  = sum(total,               na.rm=TRUE))
  
  farm_area <- farm_area_by_class %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% states, year==yr) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(small  = sum(less_than_100_ha,    na.rm=TRUE),
                     medium = sum(from_100_to_1000_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                     large  = sum(more_than_1000_ha,   na.rm=TRUE))
  
  data.frame(share_small  = 100 * farm_number$small  / farm_number$total,
             share_medium = 100 * farm_number$medium / farm_number$total,
             share_large  = 100 * farm_number$large  / farm_number$total,
             avg_small    = farm_area$small  / farm_number$small,
             avg_medium   = farm_area$medium / farm_number$medium,
             avg_large    = farm_area$large  / farm_number$large)
  
} # close get_params_3class()

# helper function to compute the 5 parameters for a given set of states and year
# small  = <2500ha
# medium = 2500-10000ha
# large  = >10000ha
get_params_5class <- function(states) {
  farm_number <- farm_table_2017 %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% states) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(small  = sum(less_than_100_ha, na.rm=TRUE) +
                              sum(from_100_to_1000_ha, na.rm=TRUE) +
                              sum(from_1000_to_2500_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                     medium = sum(from_2500_to_10000_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                     large  = sum(more_than_10000_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                     total  = sum(total, na.rm=TRUE))
  
  farm_area <- area_table_2017 %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% states) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(small  = sum(less_than_100_ha, na.rm=TRUE) +
                              sum(from_100_to_1000_ha, na.rm=TRUE) +
                              sum(from_1000_to_2500_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                     medium = sum(from_2500_to_10000_ha, na.rm=TRUE),
                     large  = sum(more_than_10000_ha, na.rm=TRUE))
  
  data.frame(share_small  = 100 * farm_number$small  / farm_number$total,
             share_medium = 100 * farm_number$medium / farm_number$total,
             share_large  = 100 * farm_number$large  / farm_number$total,
             avg_small    = farm_area$small  / farm_number$small,
             avg_medium   = farm_area$medium / farm_number$medium,
             avg_large    = farm_area$large  / farm_number$large)
    
} # closes get_params_5class()

# Table 1) Land Concentration (3 classes - 1985, 1995, 2006 and 2017)
# build wide table
years_3class <- c("1985", "1995", "2006", "2017")
# process data
rows_3class <- lapply(c(cerrado_states, "Brazil"), function(s) {
  states <- if (s == "Brazil") unique(farm_number_by_class$state) else s
  year_cols <- lapply(years_3class, function(yr) {
    df <- get_params_3class(states, yr)
    # rename columns to include year suffix before cbind
    names(df) <- paste0(names(df), "_", yr)
    df
  })
  result <- base::do.call(cbind, year_cols)
  result$area <- if (s == "Brazil") "Brazil (national)" else s
  result <- result %>% dplyr::select(area, dplyr::everything())
  result
})
# create final df
table_3class <- base::do.call(base::rbind, rows_3class)
# generate table for report
# Table 1a) Land Concentration (3 classes - 1985 and 1995)
table_3class %>%
  dplyr::select(area,
                ends_with("_1985"),
                ends_with("_1995")) %>%
  kableExtra::kable(
    col.names = c("Area",
                  rep(c("Small (%)", "Medium (%)", "Large (%)",
                        "Avg. small (ha)", "Avg. medium (ha)", "Avg. large (ha)"), 2)),
    digits  = 1,
    caption = "Land concentration parameters (small <100 ha, medium 100–1000 ha, large >1000 ha) — part 1/2"
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "1985" = 6, "1995" = 6)) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                            full_width = FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(table_3class), bold = TRUE)

# Table 1b) Land Concentration (3 classes - 2006 and 2017)
table_3class %>%
  dplyr::select(area,
                ends_with("_2006"),
                ends_with("_2017")) %>%
  kableExtra::kable(
    col.names = c("Area",
                  rep(c("Small (%)", "Medium (%)", "Large (%)",
                        "Avg. small (ha)", "Avg. medium (ha)", "Avg. large (ha)"), 2)),
    digits  = 1,
    caption = "Land concentration parameters (small <100 ha, medium 100–1000 ha, large >1000 ha) — part 2/2"
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "2006" = 6, "2017" = 6)) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                            full_width = FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(table_3class), bold = TRUE)

#table_3class %>%
#  kableExtra::kable(col.names = c("Area",
#                                  rep(c("Small (%)", "Medium (%)", "Large (%)", "Avg. small (ha)", "Avg. medium (ha)", #"Avg. large (ha)"),4)),
#                    digits  = 1,
#                    caption = "Land concentration parameters (small <100 ha, medium 100–1000 ha, large >1000 ha)") %>%
#  add_header_above(c(" " = 1,
#                     "1985" = 6, "1995" = 6,
#                     "2006" = 6, "2017" = 6)) %>%
#  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
#  row_spec(nrow(table_3class), bold = TRUE)

# Table 2) Land Concentration (3 classes - 2017)
# process data
rows_5class <- lapply(c(cerrado_states, "Brazil"), function(s) {
  states <- if (s == "Brazil") unique(farm_number_by_class_2017$state) else s
  get_params_5class(states) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(area = if (s == "Brazil") "Brazil (national)" else s,
                  .before = 1)
})
# create final df
table_5class <- base::do.call(base::rbind, rows_5class)
# generate table for report
table_5class %>%
  kableExtra::kable(col.names=c("Area", "Small (%)", "Medium (%)", "Large (%)", "Avg. small (ha)", "Avg. medium (ha)", "Avg. large (ha)"),
                    digits=1,
                    caption="Land concentration parameters (small <2500 ha, medium 2500–10000 ha, large >10000 ha)") %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                            full_width = FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(table_5class), bold=TRUE)

# clean up
rm(get_params_3class, years_3class, rows_3class, table_3class,
   get_params_5class, rows_5class, table_5class,
   area_table_2017, farm_table_2017,
   size_class_labels)

```

# Area by activity type
Using data from IBGE's Census of Agriculture (available for 1995, 2006, and 2017) at the state level, I calculate the share of farm area in each of the following classes of economic activity:
- cattle ranching;
- crop farming;
- forestry;
- other.
This data is available at the state level, but I also provide results for the whole country

```{r area_by_activity_type, echo=FALSE}

# join permanent and temporary crops
area_by_activity2 <- area_by_activity %>%
  dplyr::transmute(state, cattle,
                   crop = (perm_crop+temp_crop),
                   forestry, other, total, year)

# helper function to compute shares from a raw activity/area table
get_shares <- function(df, states) {
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::filter(state %in% states) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(year) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(cattle   = sum(cattle,   na.rm=TRUE),
                     crop     = sum(crop,     na.rm=TRUE),
                     forestry = sum(forestry, na.rm=TRUE),
                     other    = sum(other,    na.rm=TRUE),
                     total    = sum(total,    na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::transmute(year,
                     cattle   = 100 * cattle   / total,
                     crop     = 100 * crop     / total,
                     forestry = 100 * forestry / total,
                     other    = 100 * other    / total)
    return(df)
} # closes get_share()

# helper function to build wide table (states as rows, year-class as columns)
build_wide <- function(df_list) {
  # df_list: named list of per-state/national share tables
  base::do.call(base::rbind, lapply(base::names(df_list), function(nm) {
    df_list[[nm]] %>%
      tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = cattle:other,
                          names_to  = "activity_class",
                          values_to = "value") %>%
      tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from  = c(activity_class, year),
                         values_from = value) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(area = nm)
  })) %>%
    dplyr::select(
      area,
      cattle_1995, crop_1995, forestry_1995, other_1995,
      cattle_2006, crop_2006, forestry_2006, other_2006,
      cattle_2017, crop_2017, forestry_2017, other_2017)
} # close build_wide()

# Table 1) area shares
activity_area_by_state <- lapply(cerrado_states, function(s) get_shares(area_by_activity2, s))
base::names(activity_area_by_state) <- cerrado_states
area_national    <- list("Brazil" = get_shares(area_by_activity2, unique(area_by_activity2$state)))
area_wide        <- build_wide(c(activity_area_by_state, area_national))

area_wide %>%
  kableExtra::kable(col.names=c("Area",
                                "Cattle", "Crop", "Forestry", "Other",
                                "Cattle", "Crop", "Forestry", "Other",
                                "Cattle", "Crop", "Forestry", "Other"),
                    digits=1,
                    caption="Share of total farm area (%) by activity class") %>%
  kableExtra::add_header_above(c(" "=1, "1995"=4, "2006"=4, "2017"=4)) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("stripend", "hover", "condensed"),
                            full_width=FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(nrow(area_wide),bold=TRUE)

# Plot 1) stacked bar chart
# national area shares by size class (19985, 1995, 2006, 2017)
chart_data <- area_by_activity2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(year) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(cattle   = sum(cattle,   na.rm=TRUE),
                   crop     = sum(crop,     na.rm=TRUE),
                   forestry = sum(forestry, na.rm=TRUE),
                   other    = sum(other,    na.rm=TRUE),
                   total    = sum(total,    na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::transmute(year,
                   Cattle   = 100 * cattle   / total,
                   Crop     = 100 * crop     / total,
                   Forestry = 100 * forestry / total,
                   Other    = 100 * other    / total ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -year,
                      names_to  = "activity_class",
                      values_to = "share") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(activity_class = factor(activity_class,
                                        levels = c("Cattle", "Crop", "Forestry", "Other")))

g_area_by_type <- chart_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=year, y=share, fill=activity_class)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Cattle"   = "#edde8e",
                               "Crop"     = "#C27BA0",
                               "Forestry" = "#1f8d49",
                               "Other"    = "#d4271e")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  labs(title   = "National farm area by activity class",
       x       = "Census year",
       y       = "Share of total farmland area",
       fill    = "Activity class") +
  theme_minimal()

gridExtra::grid.arrange(g_area_by_type)

save_fig(g_area_by_type,"Brazil_farmland_by_activity_census")

# clean up
rm(area_by_activity2, get_shares, build_wide, activity_area_by_state, 
   area_national, area_wide, chart_data, g_area_by_type)

```

-->
